subject: Physics
title: Wormholes in Theory
description: A 60-second lesson on Wormholes in Theory.

hook.text: Einstein's equations allow tunnels through spacetime connecting distant points—called wormholes.

buildup.text: In 1935, Einstein and Rosen found that two black holes could be connected by a bridge in spacetime.

discovery.text: A wormhole could theoretically let you travel billions of light-years in an instant.

twist.text: Every known wormhole solution is unstable. They collapse faster than anything could pass through.

climax.text: Exotic matter with negative energy might hold one open, but we've never found such matter.

punchline.text: The math allows shortcuts through space. Physics says not yet.

quiz.question: What is an Einstein-Rosen bridge?
options: A theoretical tunnel connecting two distant points in spacetime
A type of light bridge between stars
